steps.title: Identify the underlined claim and Ross's evidence
steps.content: The underlined portion of Text 1 argues that volcanic activity from the Deccan Traps was the true cause of the K-Pg extinction because the eruption initially produced huge amounts of climate-altering gases. Text 2 reports Ross's team finding that Deccan gases did affect global conditions, but the climate returned to normal well before the extinctions began, and the extinctions align with the Chicxulub impact.

steps.title: Derive Ross's response
steps.content: Because the Deccan-driven climate change had already ended before the extinctions started, Ross's team would deny that those volcanic climate changes were the main driver of the K-Pg event.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — By agreeing that the Chicxulub impact changed the climate and that the Deccan Traps eruption caused the K-Pg event: overreach — Ross's team attributes the extinction to Chicxulub, not to the Deccan Traps. ✗
  • Choice B — By declaring that the changes in climate caused by the Deccan Traps eruption weren't the main cause of the K-Pg event: matches Ross's finding that the climate normalized before the extinctions, so volcanic climate change wasn't the main cause. ✓
  • Choice C — By questioning why those scientists assume that the Chicxulub impact caused the Deccan Traps eruption: misread — the Text 1 dissenters never claim the impact caused the eruption. ✗
  • Choice D — By asserting that the Deccan Traps eruption had a more significant effect on global conditions than those scientists claim: contradicts Ross, who downplays the eruption's role relative to the impact. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
